description: Machine-readable CAAS Bastion requirement map. Each row binds a known review gap to its artifact(s), its enforcing gate, a status, an explicit residual risk, and the date the binding was last verified. Consumed by audit_gate.py --external-audit-replacement (P21) to verify that every closed gap maps to a live, callable gate (or is an explicitly documented residual) rather than only to prose.

sla.note: last_verified ages on purpose: a Bastion requirement binding that has not been re-verified within the SLA window is surfaced (WARN) and eventually blocks (FAIL). Refresh by re-confirming each row's artifact+gate and bumping last_verified.

status_legend.gated: Closed AND enforced by a dedicated executable gate (an audit_gate.py sub-check or a standalone ci/*.py gate script) that verifies behavior/content, not just presence.
status_legend.presence_only: Closed deliverable whose enforcement is presence of the published document; no behavioral gate applies because the deliverable is a stance/methodology/spec narrative. Presence is enforced here by P21.
status_legend.documented_residual: An explicitly accepted/deferred residual risk (e.g. out-of-scope), tracked in docs/RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md. residual_risk MUST be non-empty.

requirements.claim: Reviewer-facing CAAS document set required to replace the verifiable parts of an external audit
requirements.residual_risk: Reviewer/reference document set; presence-gated by P21. These are narrative deliverables with no behavioral sub-gate. The bundle artifacts (EXTERNAL_AUDIT_BUNDLE.*) are additionally verified by ci/verify_external_audit_bundle.py.

requirements.claim: STRIDE threat model per exported ABI surface
RNG entropy source enumeration + attestation methodology
requirements.residual_risk: Attestation methodology document; presence-gated. The OS RNG path is validated by the integrator per the doc; no behavioral audit_gate sub-check is wired for the methodology narrative itself.

requirements.claim: Hardware side-channel (power/EM/fault) deferral methodology
requirements.residual_risk: Power/EM/microarchitectural-injection and fault-injection side channels are explicitly out of scope (deferred to operating-environment auditors) and tracked as a permanent residual in docs/RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md (gated by audit_gate.py --residual-risk-register). The methodology to lift the deferral (dudect on silicon, DPA workstation, T-test thresholds) is documented in the artifact.

requirements.claim: Cross-implementation interop matrix (libsecp256k1 / OpenSSL / Botan / Bouncy Castle / Bitcoin Core)
requirements.residual_risk: Published interop stance; presence-gated. Per-cell PASS rows reference reproducible test vectors in the document; no dedicated audit_gate sub-check aggregates them.

requirements.claim: Spec-clause to test-ID traceability (BIP-32/39/340/341/352, RFC 6979, FROST/MuSig2)
Compliance stance (FIPS/NIST/CC explicit non-claim + BIP conformance claims)
requirements.residual_risk: Compliance stance document; presence-gated. Non-claims are descriptive; the 'DO claim' rows are backed by G-5 spec-traceability evidence. No behavioral sub-check.

requirements.claim: Multi-CI reproducible-build cross-provider attestation
Two-tool constant-time independence (per-symbol tool agreement)
requirements.residual_risk: Behavioral gate is audit_gate.py --ct-tool-agreement. Artifact-based consumption of real per-tool verdict JSONs (fail-closed on a single PASS + SKIP) is ci/ct_independence_check.py; freshness wiring is Bastion B11.

requirements.claim: Citable protocol spec (algorithm suite + per-surface contracts)
requirements.residual_risk: Publishable spec document; presence-gated. Per-clause conformance is gated by G-5 (--spec-traceability); this row enforces the citable master document exists.

requirements.claim: Exploit <-> threat-model <-> spec traceability join (no in-scope high-severity clause without a PoC or residual)
Security disclosure SLA + incident timeline + RFC 9116 security.txt
External integration evidence freshness (libbitcoin / Bitcoin Core / libsecp shim) machine-tracked and gated
requirements.residual_risk: Heavy full-chain libbitcoin / Bitcoin Core surfaces are owner_gated within the manifest (explicit, never counted as current evidence); they are re-verified by an owner-imported artifact, not on every push.

requirements.claim: Constant-time evidence freshness + tool-verdict binding (CT-sensitive surfaces machine-tracked and gated)
requirements.residual_risk: Tool verdicts (ct-verif / valgrind-ct / dudect) are CI-produced and evaluated via --verdict-dir, not on every push; the committed-evidence + freshness dimension gates on push. GPU CT-uniformity is owner_gated (host-only --gpu). CT-SCALAR-INVERSE is a documented portability residual (RR-NEW-01).

requirements.claim: Fuzz campaign evidence freshness + crash->regression conversion (fuzz surfaces machine-tracked and gated)
requirements.residual_risk: Evidence-status gate only: long fuzz campaigns run in cflite/cryptofuzz/klee + the audit fuzz suite, not on push. Heavy libbitcoin differential and GPU public-data fuzz surfaces are owner_gated (explicit, never current). An unconverted crash artifact blocks regardless of severity.

requirements.claim: GPU / hardware evidence status made explicit and freshness-gated (real-device vs host-fallback vs owner-gated residual)
requirements.residual_risk: Real-device CUDA/OpenCL/Metal/ROCm evidence is owner_gated (no GitHub GPU runners; owner-run, never current on push). Fallback_correctness rows are tracked separately and are never native-performance evidence. ROCm real-device (RR-003), schnorr_snark fallback (RR-005), and hardware power/EM/fault (RR-006) are documented residuals resolving to RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md.

requirements.claim: Benchmark target-context taxonomy + claim scope (a microbenchmark cannot be mistaken for node throughput; a GPU/fallback benchmark cannot be a native-engine speed claim)
requirements.residual_risk: Folded into ci/check_bench_doc_consistency.py (--json) and co-gated by ci/perf_security_cogate.py, so a benchmark perf claim is invalid when its target_context is missing/invalid/mis-scoped or when CT/determinism gates are red. Closes residual RR-BAS-03.

requirements.claim: Research signal-matrix attack-class taxonomy + evidence routing (research intake becomes routed audit work, not just covered/candidate labels)
requirements.residual_risk: Every in-scope signal class carries an enum attack_class and routes to an expected evidence surface + gate; covered classes need existing evidence + a resolvable gate, candidates need a missing_evidence_action, out_of_scope need a rationale. Closes residual RR-BAS-04.

requirements.claim: Evidence-refresh coverage: every freshness artifact tracked by audit_sla_check.py is either auto-refreshed by a scheduled lane (verified against its commit list) or a documented residual (freshness is never trusted by hand)
requirements.residual_risk: The AUDIT_SLA.json freshness_artifacts ledger binds each tracked artifact to an `auto` producer (cross-checked against the named workflow's commit list) or a `residual` resolving to RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md; a blocking artifact with neither fails closed. Hardens (does not close) RR-BAS-01 and RR-BAS-02 - both are now ready_for_owner_promotion: ct-evidence/api-contracts remain owner-chosen documented residuals, and the incident-drill warning->blocking flip is proven safe by a self-test.

requirements.claim: Package / release provenance binding: a package or binary is trustworthy only when bound to the audited commit, the committed CAAS bundle sha256, the audit_gate verdict, and its own artifact hash (provenance binding, not release authorization)
requirements.residual_risk: Every package surface declares the full binding contract; `template` surfaces hold sentinels with null hash+run_id (no fake current evidence), `bound` surfaces must match HEAD + the committed bundle digest + a real artifact hash + verdict==pass, and `owner_gated` release artifacts are never current in the dev tree. The gate never publishes, tags, merges, or authorizes a release - release authorization stays owner-only per CLAUDE.md. Reuses existing SLSA / supply-chain infra rather than duplicating it.
